format_instructions:
For all geometry problems:
1. Give coordinates in the form (x, y)
2. Round decimal answers to 3 decimal places
3. Use the degree symbol ° for angles
4. Return only the angle, coordinates, or radius as your answer.
orthocenter_variant1: Given triangle ABC with coordinates A={A}, B={B}, and C={C}, find the coordinates of its orthocenter. {format_instructions}
orthocenter_variant2: For triangle with vertices A={A}, B={B}, and C={C}, determine the orthocenter (intersection of altitudes). {format_instructions}
incircle_radius_variant1: Consider triangle ABC with coordinates A={A}, B={B}, and C={C}. Compute the radius of its incircle. {format_instructions}
incircle_radius_variant2: Find the incircle radius of triangle ABC whose vertices are A={A}, B={B}, and C={C}. {format_instructions}
angle_measure_variant1: In triangle ABC with coordinates A={A}, B={B}, and C={C}, find the measure (in degrees) of angle ABC. {format_instructions}
angle_measure_variant2: Given a triangle with vertices A={A}, B={B}, and C={C}, determine the angle at B in degrees. {format_instructions}
